item.title: TRUMP: IF MINES ARE NOT REMOVED FORTHWITH, MILITARY CONSEQUENCES TO IRAN WILL BE AT A LEVEL NEVER SEEN BEFORE.
item.description: TRUMP: IF MINES ARE NOT REMOVED FORTHWITH, MILITARY CONSEQUENCES TO IRAN WILL BE AT A LEVEL NEVER SEEN BEFORE.

item.title: *TRUMP: IF IRAN HAS PUT MINES IN THE HORMUZ WE WANT THEM REMOVED
item.description: *TRUMP: IF IRAN HAS PUT MINES IN THE HORMUZ WE WANT THEM REMOVED

item.title:
TRUMP WARNS IRAN ON HORMUZ MINES
“If Iran has placed any mines in the Hormuz Strait—and we have no reports that they have—they must be removed immediately! Failure to do so will bring military consequences the likes of which have never been seen. If they remove them, it will be a giant step in the right direction!” — President Donald J. Trump
item.description:
TRUMP WARNS IRAN ON HORMUZ MINES

“If Iran has placed any mines in the Hormuz Strait—and we have no reports that they have—they must be removed immediately! Failure to do so will bring military consequences the likes of which have never been seen. If they remove them, it will be a giant step in the right direction!”
— President Donald J. Trump

item.title: Jury restriction plan clears first hurdle despite Labour dissent
item.description: The justice secretary says the limits are required to stem a growing backlog of cases in crown courts.

item.title: 🚨 WHITE HOUSE'S LEAVITT, ASKED ABOUT WRIGHT REMOVING POST ON TANKER ESCORTED THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ, SAYS THE U.S. HAS NOT ESCORTED VESSEL
item.description: 🚨 WHITE HOUSE'S LEAVITT, ASKED ABOUT WRIGHT REMOVING POST ON TANKER ESCORTED THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ, SAYS THE U.S. HAS NOT ESCORTED VESSEL

item.title:
SAUDI ARABIA IN TALKS TO BUY INTERCEPTOR DRONES: WSJ
Saudi Arabia is negotiating to purchase Ukrainian air-defense systems, including interceptor drones and electronic warfare, to counter Iranian Shahed drones. The deal, worth millions, is not yet finalized. Gulf states are adopting Ukraine’s drone interception and signal-jamming methods to supplement costly missile defenses, with Qatar and the U.S. also exploring similar counter-drone technologies.
item.description:
SAUDI ARABIA IN TALKS TO BUY INTERCEPTOR DRONES: WSJ

Saudi Arabia is negotiating to purchase Ukrainian air-defense systems, including interceptor drones and electronic warfare, to counter Iranian Shahed drones. The deal, worth millions, is not yet finalized. Gulf states are adopting Ukraine’s drone interception and signal-jamming methods to supplement costly missile defenses, with Qatar and the U.S. also exploring similar counter-drone technologies.

item.title: WHITE HOUSE ON IRAN: OPERATIONS WILL END WHEN TRUMP DETERMINES OBJECTIVES HAVE BEEN MET, IRAN IN UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER
item.description: WHITE HOUSE ON IRAN: OPERATIONS WILL END WHEN TRUMP DETERMINES OBJECTIVES HAVE BEEN MET, IRAN IN UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER

item.title:
🚨U.S. ASKS ISRAEL TO STOP ENERGY STRIKES
The U.S. government has urged Israel not to carry out further strikes on Iran’s energy and oil infrastructure, marking the first time it has restrained Israeli action since their joint operations began. Washington’s concerns include civilian harm, post‑war cooperation with Iran’s oil sector, and risks of broader regional retaliation that could disrupt global energy markets
item.description:
🚨U.S. ASKS ISRAEL TO STOP ENERGY STRIKES

The U.S. government has urged Israel not to carry out further strikes on Iran’s energy and oil infrastructure, marking the first time it has restrained Israeli action since their joint operations began. Washington’s concerns include civilian harm, post‑war cooperation with Iran’s oil sector, and risks of broader regional retaliation that could disrupt global energy markets

item.title: Glasgow Central's main station to remain closed until next week
item.description: A huge fire destroyed a neighbouring building but Network Rail staff believe the station has not been seriously damaged.

item.title: IRGC SPOKESPERSON DENIES U.S. CLAIM OF ESCORTING OIL TANKER IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ
item.description: IRGC SPOKESPERSON DENIES U.S. CLAIM OF ESCORTING OIL TANKER IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ

item.title:
🚨 NO U.S. ESCORTS IN HORMUZ STRAIT
According to military sources cited by Jennifer Griffin, no U.S. military assets have escorted oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, contradicting claims made by Energy Secretary Chris Wright.
item.description:
🚨 NO U.S. ESCORTS IN HORMUZ STRAIT

According to military sources cited by Jennifer Griffin, no U.S. military assets have escorted oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, contradicting claims made by Energy Secretary Chris Wright.

item.title:
TANKER TRAFFIC DROPS IN PERSIAN GULF
Morgan Stanley estimates only 3 crude/refined product tankers exited the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz today, far below the usual ~35. Tanker rates remain near historic highs despite slight cooling. Regional disruptions include Aramco and ADNOC refinery impacts, India prioritizing LPG, and potential sanction relief announced by Trump for some countries.
item.description:
TANKER TRAFFIC DROPS IN PERSIAN GULF

Morgan Stanley estimates only 3 crude/refined product tankers exited the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz today, far below the usual ~35. Tanker rates remain near historic highs despite slight cooling. Regional disruptions include Aramco and ADNOC refinery impacts, India prioritizing LPG, and potential sanction relief announced by Trump for some countries.

*Walter Bloomberg (@DeItaone)

item.title: TRUMP ORDER CUTTING TIES WITH ANTHROPIC LIKELY COMING LATER THIS WEEK- CBS NEWS
item.description: TRUMP ORDER CUTTING TIES WITH ANTHROPIC LIKELY COMING LATER THIS WEEK- CBS NEWS

item.title:
IRAN SHIPS OIL THROUGH HORMUZ DESPITE STRIKES
A supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iranian oil passed through the Strait of Hormuz to China, marking the sixth Iranian vessel since February 28. At least 11 million barrels have been shipped to Asia amid ongoing U.S.-Israeli strikes, with Iran also moving gas and oil products through the critical waterway.
item.description:
IRAN SHIPS OIL THROUGH HORMUZ DESPITE STRIKES

A supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iranian oil passed through the Strait of Hormuz to China, marking the sixth Iranian vessel since February 28. At least 11 million barrels have been shipped to Asia amid ongoing U.S.-Israeli strikes, with Iran also moving gas and oil products through the critical waterway.

item.title: Nottingham triple killer assaulted husband and wife weeks before attacks, inquiry told
item.description: Valdo Calocane attacked two colleagues weeks before he killed three people in Nottingham.

item.title: HMS Dragon sets sail from Portsmouth and heads to Cyprus
item.description: The UK has deployed the Type 45 Destroyer to the Mediterranean region, after an Iranian drone struck the runway at the RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus on 1 March.

item.title: FOUR IRANIAN DIPLOMATS KILLED IN LEBANON ON SUNDAY IN ISRAELI ATTACK - NOURNEWS CITING DIPLOMAT
item.description: FOUR IRANIAN DIPLOMATS KILLED IN LEBANON ON SUNDAY IN ISRAELI ATTACK - NOURNEWS CITING DIPLOMAT

item.title:
HORMUZ CLOSURE CRIPPLES FOOD SECURITY
UN Trade and Development warns the Strait of Hormuz disruption—traffic down 97%—is spiking energy, fertilizer, transport, and insurance costs, echoing Covid-19 and Ukraine war shocks.
With 20% of global oil/LNG and one-third of seaborne fertilizer (16 million tonnes in 2024) routed through the strait, food prices surge hardest on vulnerable developing nations facing debt burdens and soaring borrowing costs. Poorest countries like Sudan, Somalia, and others face acute fertilizer shortages.
item.description:
HORMUZ CLOSURE CRIPPLES FOOD SECURITY

UN Trade and Development warns the Strait of Hormuz disruption—traffic down 97%—is spiking energy, fertilizer, transport, and insurance costs, echoing Covid-19 and Ukraine war shocks.

With 20% of global oil/LNG and one-third of seaborne fertilizer (16 million tonnes in 2024) routed through the strait, food prices surge hardest on vulnerable developing nations facing debt burdens and soaring borrowing costs. Poorest countries like Sudan, Somalia, and others face acute fertilizer shortages.

item.title:
ISRAEL TO BOOST DEFENSE BUDGET $13B FOR IRAN CONFLICT
Israel plans to increase its defense spending by ~40 billion shekels ($13B), about 2% of GDP, to fund the war with Iran, according to a finance official. The supplement is part of the 2026 revised budget, set for cabinet debate and possible approval Tuesday under PM Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.description:
ISRAEL TO BOOST DEFENSE BUDGET $13B FOR IRAN CONFLICT

Israel plans to increase its defense spending by ~40 billion shekels ($13B), about 2% of GDP, to fund the war with Iran, according to a finance official. The supplement is part of the 2026 revised budget, set for cabinet debate and possible approval Tuesday under PM Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title:
BRENT OIL TO STAY HIGH SHORT-TERM, SLIDE BELOW $80 BY YEAR-END – EIA
🔸Brent oil likely above $95/bbl over the next 2 months due to Middle East tensions; expected to drop to $70/bbl by end-2026.
🔸U.S. oil production projected at 13.6 mln bpd in 2026, rising to 13.8 mln bpd in 2027.
🔸Strait of Hormuz disruption will cut Middle East output temporarily; gradual recovery expected.
🔸U.S. gasoline prices forecast at $3.34/gal in 2026, $3.18/gal in 2027.
🔸OPEC+ unlikely to boost production significantly next year; global supply to exceed demand once Hormuz flows resume.
🔸Global inventories set to grow by 1.9 mln bpd in 2026 and 3.0 mln bpd in 2027.
item.description:
BRENT OIL TO STAY HIGH SHORT-TERM, SLIDE BELOW $80 BY YEAR-END – EIA

🔸Brent oil likely above $95/bbl over the next 2 months due to Middle East tensions; expected to drop to $70/bbl by end-2026.

🔸U.S. oil production projected at 13.6 mln bpd in 2026, rising to 13.8 mln bpd in 2027.

🔸Strait of Hormuz disruption will cut Middle East output temporarily; gradual recovery expected.

🔸U.S. gasoline prices forecast at $3.34/gal in 2026, $3.18/gal in 2027.

🔸OPEC+ unlikely to boost production significantly next year; global supply to exceed demand once Hormuz flows resume.

🔸Global inventories set to grow by 1.9 mln bpd in 2026 and 3.0 mln bpd in 2027.

item.title:
RAND PAUL WARNS HIGH OIL COULD HURT GOP IN MIDTERMS
Rand Paul warned Republicans could face a “disastrous” midterm election if oil prices stay high amid the conflict with Iran.
The senator criticized the rationale for U.S. strikes and said prolonged military action pushing oil above $100—and raising gasoline prices—could create major political headwinds for the Republican Party.
item.description:
RAND PAUL WARNS HIGH OIL COULD HURT GOP IN MIDTERMS

Rand Paul warned Republicans could face a “disastrous” midterm election if oil prices stay high amid the conflict with Iran.

The senator criticized the rationale for U.S. strikes and said prolonged military action pushing oil above $100—and raising gasoline prices—could create major political headwinds for the Republican Party.

item.title: WITKOFF: RUSSIANS SAID THEY HAVE NOT BEEN SHARING INTEL W/ IRAN
item.description: WITKOFF: RUSSIANS SAID THEY HAVE NOT BEEN SHARING INTEL W/ IRAN

item.title:
PSILOCYBIN BEATS NICOTINE PATCHES IN QUITTING SMOKING
A study published in JAMA Network Open found a single dose of psilocybin—the active compound in psychedelic mushrooms—was far more effective than nicotine patches at helping smokers quit.
In a trial of 82 adults, over 40% of participants who received psilocybin were smoke-free after six months, compared with about 10% using nicotine patches, with both groups receiving therapy.
Lead researcher Matthew Johnson of Johns Hopkins University said the drug may help by altering brain communication and self-perception, potentially breaking addictive patterns.
The findings add momentum to growing research into psychedelic-assisted treatments, though larger trials are still needed.
item.description:
PSILOCYBIN BEATS NICOTINE PATCHES IN QUITTING SMOKING

A study published in JAMA Network Open found a single dose of psilocybin—the active compound in psychedelic mushrooms—was far more effective than nicotine patches at helping smokers quit.

In a trial of 82 adults, over 40% of participants who received psilocybin were smoke-free after six months, compared with about 10% using nicotine patches, with both groups receiving therapy.

Lead researcher Matthew Johnson of Johns Hopkins University said the drug may help by altering brain communication and self-perception, potentially breaking addictive patterns.

The findings add momentum to growing research into psychedelic-assisted treatments, though larger trials are still needed.

item.title: WITKOFF: LET'S SEE IF IRANIANS WANT TO TALK
item.description: WITKOFF: LET'S SEE IF IRANIANS WANT TO TALK

item.title:
IRAN CLAIMS WAR MOMENTUM, 90% STRIKE SUCCESS
Iran says the war is shifting in its favor. Ebrahim Rezaei, spokesman for the Iranian parliament’s security commission, claimed missile and drone strikes have a 90% success rate, though he offered no evidence.
Tehran also said it is not negotiating with the U.S., despite signals from Donald Trump that talks could be possible.
item.description:
IRAN CLAIMS WAR MOMENTUM, 90% STRIKE SUCCESS

Iran says the war is shifting in its favor. Ebrahim Rezaei, spokesman for the Iranian parliament’s security commission, claimed missile and drone strikes have a 90% success rate, though he offered no evidence.

Tehran also said it is not negotiating with the U.S., despite signals from Donald Trump that talks could be possible.
